checks: When opening the game in a browser with only one human player, NPC bots automatically appear in the lobby and the match starts with bots filling the slots
NPC bots move around the arena autonomously during active gameplay — they navigate toward other players, shoot at nearby enemies, and move away from the shrinking ring boundary
NPC bots can be shot and eliminated by the human player just like real opponents — their HP decreases and they die at 0 HP
NPC bot name tags display a '[BOT]' prefix and are rendered in orange (#f80) color, clearly distinguishing them from real players whose names are gray (#aaa)
The lobby HUD info text shows bot count separately (e.g., '1 player + 3 bots in lobby') when bots are present
When inspecting the WebSocket game state messages, each NPC player object includes an 'isNPC: true' field
All existing 877 tests continue to pass when running npm test, and new NPC-specific tests also pass
